name: United Labor
description_short: Federally insured state-chartered credit union in Raytown, MO serving 5,479 members with $21.17M in assets since 1987.
description_long:
United Labor Credit Union is a federally insured state-chartered credit union headquartered in Raytown, Missouri, established in 1987. The institution has grown to serve approximately 5,479 members with total assets of $21.17 million as of March 2025. It operates a single branch location at 10000 E 66th Terrace in Raytown and employs 5 full and part-time staff members under the leadership of CEO Tim Vogler and Chairperson Mike Bynum.

The credit union offers fundamental banking and financial services including checking and savings accounts, ATM and debit card programs, and various loan products. Their loan portfolio consists of real estate loans ($190,153), unsecured loans ($2.11 million), and other loans totaling $9.48 million, representing $11.78 million in total lending. Member deposits are NCUSIF-insured, providing federal protection up to applicable limits.

United Labor operates as a state-chartered natural person credit union without FHLB membership. The institution maintains conservative financial practices, with recent financial statements showing loan income of $743,755 and investment income of $241,071. Operating expenses totaled $346,168, with net income of $115,675 reported for the most recent period.

As a small community credit union, United Labor serves a localized membership base in the Kansas City metropolitan area. The institution represents a traditional credit union model focused on member service rather than aggressive growth. Their modest asset size and single branch location limit operational reach compared to larger regional competitors. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Federally insured through NCUSIF, providing deposit protection for members
Member-owned cooperative structure with democratic governance
Offers low-cost wire transfers as a service advantage
39-year operational history demonstrating institutional stability since 1987
ATM and debit card access programs for member convenience
Net positive earnings of $115,675 showing financial stability
Local Raytown headquarters with direct community accountability
cons: Single branch location limits accessibility for members outside Raytown area
Small asset base ($21.17M) compared to regional credit unions, potentially limiting product offerings
No FHLB membership may restrict capital availability and lending capacity
Modest loan portfolio suggests limited specialty lending products or competitive rates for larger loans
Low investment income ($241,071) indicates minimal investment diversification
best_for: Local Raytown and greater Kansas City area residents seeking community-based banking
Members prioritizing member-owned cooperative structure over corporate banking
Individuals needing basic checking, savings, and ATM/debit card services
diagnosis: United Labor Credit Union is best suited for Raytown and Kansas City-area residents seeking traditional community credit union banking with deposit insurance protection. The primary caveat is the single-branch operation, which may be inconvenient for members outside the immediate Raytown area, and the modest asset size may limit product variety compared to larger regional institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
